detail.msg: Input should be a valid boolean

BadRequest.description: The request body failed validation (malformed JSON, wrong type, or unknown field).

Forbidden.description: The `x-api-key` is invalid or revoked (authorizer Deny / API key rejected). Gateway-level — the body is `{ message }`, not the canonical envelope.

InternalError.description: Unexpected server error. The Lambda returns the canonical envelope; a rare infrastructure-level 5xx from API Gateway instead carries only `{ message }`.

NotFound.description: No pipeline with that id exists for the authenticated org.

subscription.summary: Application — trial expired / no active subscription

value.message: Your trial period is over. Please create a subscription.

RunForbidden.description: Two distinct 403 causes: a gateway-level auth failure (invalid/revoked API key — `{ message }`), OR an application-level rejection when the org is outside its paid/trial window (canonical `{ success: false, message }`).

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit exceeded (enforced by the API gateway usage plan). Gateway-level — the body is `{ message }`, not the canonical envelope.

Unauthorized.description: No `x-api-key` was supplied, or the authorizer could not resolve it. Gateway-level — the body is `{ message }`, not the canonical envelope.

ErrorEnvelope.description: Canonical error envelope. `success` is always false; `message` is always present.

detail.description: Structured per-field errors; present on 400 validation failures.

error_code.description: Machine-readable error identifier for branching without parsing `message`.

GatewayError.description: API Gateway-level error body (authorizer deny, invalid/missing API key, throttling) — produced before the Lambda runs. Carries only `message`, NOT the canonical `{success, ...}` envelope.

info.description:
Public, API-key-authenticated API for triggering and terminating Analitiq data-pipeline runs.

## Authentication
Every request must carry the org's Data Sync API key in the `x-api-key` header. Keys are minted/revoked from the Analitiq settings UI.

## Response format
Application responses (handled by the Lambda) use the canonical envelope `{ success, message?, data? }`: success bodies (`success: true`) carry endpoint-specific `data`; error bodies (`success: false`) carry `message` and optionally `error_code` / `detail`. Absent optional fields are omitted from the wire, never null. The request and success-response payloads are published as versioned JSON Schema contracts under `https://schemas.analitiq.ai/data-sync-*/latest.json` and referenced below.

**Gateway-level rejections** (authentication `401`/`403` and throttling `429`) are produced by API Gateway BEFORE the Lambda runs and carry a different, smaller body — `{ "message": ... }` only, with no `success` field. Validate auth/throttle failures against the `GatewayError` shape, not the canonical envelope.

## Throttling
Requests are throttled per org (steady 1 req/s, burst 5). Exceeding the limit returns `429` with the gateway `{ message }` body.

## Changelog
- **2026-06-24 — ADDED**: a run-status endpoint, `GET /pipelines/{pipeline_id}/runs/{invocation_id}`, returns a run's outcome by `invocation_id` (the id from the run endpoint's `202`): a coarse `status` (`queued`/`running`/`succeeded`/`partial`/`failed`/`cancelled`), record counts, timestamps, and — on `failed`/`partial` — a customer-safe `error` category. Published as the `data-sync-run-status` contract. This is the public way to poll the outcome of an accepted asynchronous run; no existing endpoint changed.
- **2026-06-23 — BREAKING**: the run endpoint is now asynchronous. It returns `202 Accepted` with `data` = `{ invocation_id, pipeline_id }` instead of `200 OK` with the submitted job (`job_id`, `job_name`); the AWS Batch job is dispatched in the background so the request never blocks on the gateway 29-second limit. Poll run history by `invocation_id` for the final outcome. The run success-response contract moved from `data-sync-run-response` to `data-sync-run-accepted`. Terminate is unchanged.
- **2026-06-12 — BREAKING**: run/terminate responses now use the canonical `{ success, message?, data? }` envelope. The action fields (`job_id`, `job_name`, `pipeline_id`, `invocation_id`) moved from the top level into `data`; an idempotent terminate with nothing to stop omits `data` entirely (distinguished by `message`). Clients that read the top-level action fields must read them from `data` instead. This is a `v1` in-place breaking change — there is no compatibility shim.
info.title: Analitiq Data Sync API

post.description: Accepts a pipeline run for asynchronous execution. The request is validated synchronously (org, billing, pipeline state, duplicate check); on success it returns `202 Accepted` with an `invocation_id` while the AWS Batch job is submitted in the background, so the call never blocks on the gateway's 29-second limit. Poll run history by `invocation_id` for the final outcome. If a run is already in flight and `terminate_existing_sync` is false (default), the request is rejected with `409`.

requestBody.description: Optional run options. An empty body is valid.

202.description: Run accepted. The AWS Batch job is dispatched asynchronously; poll run history by `invocation_id` for the final outcome.

409.description: A run is already in flight for this pipeline and `terminate_existing_sync` was false.

get.description: Returns the status of a single run by `invocation_id` — the id handed back by the run endpoint's `202 Accepted`. Poll this to learn whether an accepted run is `queued`, `running`, or has reached a terminal state (`succeeded`, `partial`, `failed`, `cancelled`), along with record counts, timestamps, and — on `failed`/`partial` — a customer-safe error category. Scoped to the authenticated org and the pipeline in the path: an unknown run, a run owned by another org, or a run that belongs to a different pipeline all return `404`.

parameters.description: The pipeline the run belongs to.
parameters.name: pipeline_id

parameters.description: The run identifier returned by the run endpoint's 202 accept.
parameters.name: invocation_id

failed.summary: Run failed with a customer-safe error category

error.message: Authentication to the data source failed. Check the source connection credentials.

400.description: The `invocation_id` path parameter is not a valid id.

404.description: No run with that `invocation_id` exists for the authenticated org and pipeline.

get.summary: Get the status of a pipeline run

post.description: Stops the current run for the pipeline. Idempotent: when there is nothing to stop (no run recorded, run already terminal), it still returns `200` with `success: true` and no `data`, distinguished by `message`. No request body is expected.

200.description: Terminate resolved (a running job was stopped, or there was nothing to stop).
